concerns.text: FR-004 mandates a 'Group-Level paired-sample t-test'. The tasks (T021, T022) implement a 'one-sample t-test' (per plan.md). No task exists to implement the paired-sample t-test required by the spec. This is a missing task for a functional requirement.

FR-001 mandates downloading 'ds000115'. Tasks T004, T012 implement download of 'ds000246'. No task exists to download the dataset specified in the spec (ds000115). The spec and plan are contradictory; a task for the spec's requirement is missing.

SC-002 requires 'At least one brain cluster survives FDR' as a success criterion. Tasks T021-T024 implement logic to handle 'NULL RESULT' (no clusters) gracefully. No task exists to enforce or validate the specific success criterion of 'at least one cluster' if that were a hard requirement, or to validate the spec's contradictory success condition against the plan's null-result handling.

Task T004 references 'ds000246' which does not exist in spec.md (which cites ds000115). This is a stale tag/reference relative to the current spec document, indicating the task was written for a corrected plan but the spec was not updated. The tag should be removed or the spec updated to match.

Task T021 implements 'one-sample t-test' while spec FR-004 requires 'paired-sample t-test'. The task description does not tag the specific FR it satisfies (no [FR-004] tag), and the implementation contradicts the spec. This is a stale/incorrect mapping relative to the spec.
